name: stale-ticket-no-data
description: Ticket is 3 days old. ClickHouse retention only 48 hours. Cannot verify. No current anomalies.

item.title: Historical error spike on customer-support-bot (3 days ago)
item.description: Automated alert from 3 days ago: error rate exceeded 25% on customer-support-bot between 02:00-04:00 UTC.
item.note:
Attempted to investigate the 3-day-old error spike. Hit a data limitation:

- The alert was generated on 2024-01-12 at 02:15 UTC
- ClickHouse retention policy: 48 hours for raw trace data
- Current time: 2024-01-15 10:30 UTC — the relevant data was purged ~24 hours ago
- Queries for traces between 2024-01-12 02:00-04:00 UTC return empty results
- Aggregated hourly stats are also past retention (kept for 72h, expired ~6h ago)

What I CAN see (current state):
- customer-support-bot last 24h: 195 traces, 1.5% error rate — within baseline
- email-classifier last 24h: 52 traces, 0% error rate — normal
- No active alerts or anomalies
- Latency normal across both agents
- No deployment events in the last 4 days per git log

Cannot verify or disprove the historical alert. The system is currently healthy. If this was a transient issue, it appears to have resolved. If it was an early indicator, there's no current evidence of recurrence.

Recommendation: close as unverifiable due to data retention limits. If the alert pattern recurs, it will generate a new ticket with fresh data.

projectMemory:
## Project Agents
- **customer-support-bot**: Handles customer inquiries via RAG. Typical trace has 3-5 LLM calls, 1-2 retrieval steps. Normal latency: 2-4s per trace.
- **email-classifier**: Classifies incoming emails. Single LLM call per trace, ~500ms.

## Baselines
- Error rate: <2%
- Daily cost: ~$5
- Trace volume: ~200/day
